issues.body: The check named `bundle leaves the worker tree pristine for the gate (index reset)` only reads `mod.txt` and expects `new`. That result is identical whether or not `worker_bundle` resets the index, so deleting the `git reset` line would leave this security-critical test green. The assertion therefore does not test the invariant it names.
issues.fix_hint: After running `bundle`, assert that the index is clean, for example by checking that `git -C "$bsrc" diff --cached --quiet` succeeds, while separately retaining checks that the intended working-tree edits still exist. Ensure the assertion turns red if the reset is removed.
issues.title: Index-reset self-test is vacuous

issues.body: The self-test commentary says a DIRTY destination tree is proved to fail closed, and `reconstruct_bundle` contains a load-bearing cleanliness check, but the added cases cover only digest tampering and a moved base. There is no reconstruction attempt against a checkout with an uncommitted or untracked change, nor an assertion that the patch was not applied. A regression removing or weakening the dirty-tree guard would pass the suite.
issues.fix_hint: Create a fresh clone at the expected base, add an uncommitted/untracked file, invoke the real `reconstruct` command with a valid digest, assert a nonzero result, and assert that none of the bundled changes were applied.
issues.title: Dirty-checkout fail-closed path has no test

summary: The clean-runner, digest-bound publish split is directionally sound and the runtime reconstruction checks fail closed. However, the new trust-plane self-test contains a vacuous assertion for the load-bearing index reset and claims dirty-tree coverage that it never implements. Because these tests can remain green after weakening important handoff invariants, the change is not yet approvable.
